BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick break-even check for Hassan's two side hustles: the lo-fi beat shop and the Southern recipe-card download store. Goal is to see how many $5 beats and $4 recipe cards he needs to sell each month just to cover the small fixed online costs while balancing dining-hall shifts and classes.

CurrentState.Note: Fixed costs are the domain (~$1.25/mo prorated), website hosting ($12/mo), and Stripe processing fees plus a little Canva and sample-pack spend. Revenue is roughly 9 beats at $5 and 5 recipe cards at $4 in an average month.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Add storefront hosting upgrade
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $34 / ($5 beat - $0.45 Stripe fee) = ~7.5, round up to 8 beats per month
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Adding a paid storefront hosting upgrade pushes fixed costs to ~$34/month; needs about 8 beat sales to break even.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Add monthly social ad spend
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Running a $10/month social ad bumps fixed costs to ~$44/month; needs about 10 beat sales to break even.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: With about $28/month in fixed online costs, Hassan only needs to sell roughly 6 beats (at $5) OR 7 recipe cards (at $4) each month to break even. Everything above that goes toward textbooks and savings.
ActionItems: Release 4 new lo-fi beats per month to keep the shop active
Bundle 3 recipe cards as a $12 pack to lift average order value
Move beat shop out of Stripe test mode before the fall semester
